description:
Backup operators regularly test that a snapshot copied from a remote site can be restored locally without data loss.  
  You already have a miniature snapshot staged in the directory  

      /home/user/backup_source

  Your goal is to simulate a restore test for that snapshot and record a verification log.

  1. Copy the entire contents of /home/user/backup_source – **preserving its internal directory structure** – into a brand-new directory named  

         /home/user/backup_restore  

     The restore directory must end up with exactly the same files and sub-directories found in the source.

  2. After the copy completes, create a directory called  

         /home/user/backup_logs  

     (create it only if it does not yet exist).  
     Inside that directory create a plain-text log file named  

         /home/user/backup_logs/2023-restore-test.log

  3. The log file **must follow this precise, two-part format** (including capitalisation, spacing and the vertical bar “|” delimiter) so that automated grading can validate it:

     Line 1 (header):  
         RESTORE VERIFICATION LOG

     Subsequent lines (one per restored file, sorted in *alphabetical order by relative path*):  

         <relative path>|<SHA256 checksum>

     Example layout (the real paths and checksums will obviously differ):
         RESTORE VERIFICATION LOG  
         docs/example.txt|d2d2d2…  
         images/photo.png|e3e3e3…

     • “relative path” starts directly after “backup_source/” (e.g. docs/report.txt).  
     • The checksum must be the exact lowercase hex string produced by the command “sha256sum <file>”.  
     • Use a single UNIX newline (“\n”) after every line, including the last one.

  4. When everything is complete, print the single line

         RESTORE COMPLETED

     to standard output so the operator (and the grader) knows the run is finished.

  A correct solution therefore results in:
    • /home/user/backup_restore containing an exact duplicate of /home/user/backup_source  
    • /home/user/backup_logs/2023-restore-test.log existing and matching the required format and content  
    • “RESTORE COMPLETED” appearing in the terminal output
truth:
<!--
      The snapshot that already exists before the agent starts.
  -->
  <initial_state>
    <directory path="/home/user/backup_source" mode="755">
      <directory path="/home/user/backup_source/docs" mode="755">
        <file path="/home/user/backup_source/docs/report.txt" mode="644">
          <content>hello world</content>
          <sha256>b94d27b9934d3e08a52e52d7da7dabfac484efe37a5380ee9088f7ace2efcde9</sha256>
        </file>
      </directory>
      <directory path="/home/user/backup_source/images" mode="755">
        <file path="/home/user/backup_source/images/logo.txt" mode="644">
          <content>abc</content>
          <sha256>ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad</sha256>
        </file>
      </directory>
    </directory>
    <!-- backup_logs and backup_restore do NOT exist yet -->
  </initial_state>

  <expected_state_after_agent>
    <!-- directory should be recreated -->
    <directory path="/home/user/backup_restore" mode="755">
      <directory path="/home/user/backup_restore/docs" mode="755">
        <file path="/home/user/backup_restore/docs/report.txt" mode="644">
          <content>hello world</content>
          <sha256>b94d27b9934d3e08a52e52d7da7dabfac484efe37a5380ee9088f7ace2efcde9</sha256>
        </file>
      </directory>
      <directory path="/home/user/backup_restore/images" mode="755">
        <file path="/home/user/backup_restore/images/logo.txt" mode="644">
          <content>abc</content>
          <sha256>ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad</sha256>
        </file>
      </directory>
    </directory>

    <directory path="/home/user/backup_logs" mode="755">
      <file path="/home/user/backup_logs/2023-restore-test.log" mode="644">
        <content>
RESTORE VERIFICATION LOG
docs/report.txt|b94d27b9934d3e08a52e52d7da7dabfac484efe37a5380ee9088f7ace2efcde9
images/logo.txt|ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad
        </content>
      </file>
    </directory>
  </expected_state_after_agent>

  <!--
      The grading script will:
        • Verify the destination directory hierarchy and file contents.
        • Open the log file and compare it byte-for-byte with the <content> above.
        • Check that the runtime STDOUT contains exactly one occurrence of
              RESTORE COMPLETED
  -->
name: task_000000_f1aa08d8
